pattern: Reconstruct the shortest string containing both inputs as subsequences by walking the longest-common-subsequence table and emitting shared and unshared characters in order.
hints: The shortest supersequence keeps every character of both strings but shares the characters they have in common, so its length is len1 + len2 minus their longest common subsequence.
Compute the LCS length table, then trace back from the bottom-right deciding at each cell which characters to append.
On a match, emit that character once and move diagonally; otherwise emit the character from whichever string the larger neighbor came from, then reverse and flush any leftover prefixes.

edge_inputs.description: first example with overlap on both ends
identical strings need no extra characters
no common characters means full concatenation length
one string is a subsequence of the other

stress.description: random short strings over a tiny alphabet so the LCS overlap is substantial
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alpha = 'abc'
    a = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(rng.randrange(1, n + 1)))
    b = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(rng.randrange(1, n + 1)))
    return [a, b]
stress.note: small alphabet maximizes shared subsequences; validator accepts any minimal result
